decision: Add scripts/deploy.sh (phased: preflight->capture->build/pull->db->migrate->up->health->done) + scripts/deploy-lib.sh (sourceable, unit-testable helpers). Target selection is CONFIG: deploy/env/<target>.env (kinsale/muckross/cloud). Replaces hard-coded package.json deploy:kinsale/deploy:muckross with a single `deploy` script. deploy-local.sh + rebuild.sh left UNCHANGED (still the daemon-consolidation/single-container helpers).
rationale: F9 spec + AC1-5. Env-file selection makes adding a target config-only. All external effects route through DOCKER_BIN/CURL_BIN/TCP_CHECK overrides so the phases are testable without a cluster.

decision: Stamp DAAX_DEPLOY_BY/VIA/MODE/HOST (NOT the spec's aspirational DAAX_DEPLOYED_BY/VIA) and pass them through the compose daax service.
rationale: lib/build/build-info.ts (the live F8 Build page) reads DAAX_DEPLOY_BY/VIA/MODE/HOST. Wiring the real consumed names is what actually surfaces provenance; the DAAX_DEPLOYED_* spelling in brain2daax.md is spec-only and unused in code.

decision: do_rollback is a no-op unless phase_capture ran (CAPTURED flag), not merely `[[ -f STATEFILE ]]` (mktemp pre-creates it).
rationale: Premortem: a preflight failure (e.g. missing secret) must leave the RUNNING production stack untouched. The -f check would have hit the 'fresh deploy' branch and run `compose down`, taking a live site offline on a misconfig. Locked in by a negative test asserting no `compose down` on preflight failure.

decision: Support managed Postgres via DATABASE_URL connection-string swap (DAAX_PG_MANAGED=1, preflight TCP reachability, fail-closed). Ship deploy/iac/cloud as a documented, provider-parameterized SKELETON (variables.tf contract + inert main.tf) — full IaC and a managed-only compose override are marked follow-ups per AC5 ('do NOT half-build broken Terraform').
rationale: Local needs no IaC; cloud is the same Compose stack on any VM (zero lock-in). The swap mechanism is real today; the resource graph is deliberately deferred.

decision: Gate the rollback teardown on a POSITIVE pre-mutation `compose ps -aq daax terminal` check (STACK_EXISTED_AT_CAPTURE), not on a single docker-inspect image-id capture. compose down fires ONLY when a stack positively did not exist at capture; a present-but-no-baseline stack (transient inspect miss) is left untouched (degraded, manual). A failed `compose ps` biases to 'present' (never tear down on doubt).
rationale: A momentarily-absent/restarting/stopped prod container made had_prior_state=false, so any later failure ran `compose down` and took a live stack offline. -a includes stopped containers, so temporarily-down prod is now classed as upgrade and never torn down.

decision: Track SWITCHED (set at phase_up start). A pre-up failure (build/db/migrate) restores :latest tags for hygiene but does NOT force-recreate the app plane; only in/after phase_up does rollback actively restore+recreate.
rationale: On an upgrade, a pre-up failure never switched the running stack; force-recreating it caused needless downtime.

topic: H2 cloud pull path — primary-source correction (review fix)
decision: Parametrize compose image refs (${DAAX_IMAGE:-daax:latest}, ${DAAX_TERMINAL_IMAGE:-daax-terminal:latest}) so they are live knobs and rollback tags match deployed refs. Default cloud.env to BUILD-ON-TARGET (DAAX_DEPLOY_PULL=0); removed the broken DAAX_IMAGE pull config.
rationale: Reviewer stated gh-100 publishes ghcr.io/daax-dev/daax-terminal; primary source (.github/workflows/publish-images.yml) publishes only daax-web (target runner) + code-server — NO daax-terminal image. The split needs both, so pull cannot work yet. Documented as follow-up (publish daax-terminal). A mistaken pull now fails closed at the build phase, not a silent wrong-image deploy.

decision: M2: flock guard warns LOUDLY when serialization cannot engage (missing flock or DAAX_DEPLOY_NO_LOCK=1). M3: happy-path test asserts phase ORDER via match indices. M4: added post-up health-failure rollback tests. L1: assert_required_secrets strips ALL whitespace ([[:space:]]) so a tab/newline-only secret fails the fail-closed gate.
rationale: Close the remaining medium/low review findings; keep the fail-closed gates non-fool-open.
